26.reason: [Incorrect format specifier: expects a char between two ints, but input has space-separated integers without any delimiter character to read into ch1]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect format specifier: expects a char between two ints, but input has space-separated integers without any delimiter character to read into ch1]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect format specifier: expects a space between coefficient and exponent, but %c reads the space as ch1; should use " %d %d" instead]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect scanf format: expects coefficient, then a character (like space), then exponent, but input has space-separated integers without any delimiter character between them; this will cause parsing failure or undefined behavior]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect format specifier: %d%c%d expects a digit, then a single char (like space), then another digit. But input has space between coefficient and exponent, so %c reads the space correctly, however this approach is fragile and may fail if multiple spaces exist. More critically, the program never initializes cur1->next to NULL before possibly using it, leading to undefined behavior when traversing the list later.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Incorrect format specifier: expects a space between coefficient and exponent, but %c reads the space as ch1; should use "%d %d" instead]

43.reason: [Same issue: %c reads the space as a character, causing misalignment in input parsing]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: %c reads the space as a character, causing misalignment in input parsing]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue: %c consumes the space, leading to incorrect parsing of input]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Same incorrect scanf format as above; should be "%d %d" to read two integers separated by whitespace]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Same issue as above: fragile input parsing and uninitialized next pointer before assignment in some cases. Also, after the last term, cur2->next remains uninitialized because the loop breaks before setting it to NULL, causing undefined behavior during traversal.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Same error as above: incorrect scanf format for reading two integers separated by space]
